items.title: Walk a document tree
items.scenario: A Markdown spec is parsed into Title → Sections → Sub-sections → Paragraphs; you want every leaf paragraph.
items.explanation: A recursive walk reads 1 line of code per case ('if leaf yield, else recurse into children').

items.title: Beam search / tree-of-thought
items.scenario: An LLM expands the top-k partial hypotheses at each step.
items.explanation: Each expansion is a recursive call on the next layer, often with pruning. Iterative loops work too, but the recursive form mirrors the tree directly.

items.title: JSON / config validation
items.scenario: Validate a deeply nested config (lists of dicts of dicts).
items.explanation: A recursive validator walks every key; the base case is a primitive leaf.

items.title: Dynamic programming for edit distance
items.scenario: Compute Levenshtein distance between a generated answer and the gold reference for eval.
items.explanation: Naive recursion is O(3ᵐⁿ); add memoization (or build a DP table) and it's O(m·n).

items.title: When NOT to recurse
items.scenario: Walk a chain of 100,000 retrieved chunks linked by `.next` to assemble a long reading trail.
items.explanation: Recursing 100k deep blows past Python's stack. Convert to a `while node: ... node = node.next` loop.
